Culture_Industry_Reconsidered::1.title: Culture_Industry_Reconsidered

Theodor_W._Adorno::8.title: Theodor_W._Adorno
Theodor_W._Adorno::8.text: Amidst the vogue enjoyed by existentialism and positivism in early 20th-century Europe , Adorno advanced a dialectical conception of natural history that critiqued the twin temptations of ontology and empiricism through studies of Kierkegaard and Husserl .

Dialectic_of_Enlightenment::5.title: Dialectic_of_Enlightenment
Dialectic_of_Enlightenment::5.text: Together with The Authoritarian Personality -LRB- 1950 ; also co-authored by Adorno -RRB- and Frankfurt School member Herbert Marcuse 's One-Dimensional Man -LRB- 1964 -RRB- , it has had a major effect on 20th-century philosophy , sociology , culture , and politics , inspiring especially the New Left of the 1960s and 1970s .

Theodor_W._Adorno::5.title: Theodor_W._Adorno
Theodor_W._Adorno::5.text: As a critic of both fascism and what he called the culture industry , his writings -- such as Dialectic of Enlightenment -LRB- 1947 -RRB- , Minima Moralia -LRB- 1951 -RRB- and Negative Dialectics -LRB- 1966 -RRB- -- strongly influenced the European New Left .

Theodor_W._Adorno::15.title: Theodor_W._Adorno
Theodor_W._Adorno::15.text: Adorno 's posthumously published Aesthetic Theory , which he planned to dedicate to Samuel Beckett , is the culmination of a lifelong commitment to modern art which attempts to revoke the `` fatal separation '' of feeling and understanding long demanded by the history of philosophy and explode the privilege aesthetics accords to content over form and contemplation over immersion .

Karl_Marx::6.title: Karl_Marx
